If you can believe the lie that you, as a single human being, are somehow completely independent and separate from the people that surround you, there are a whole bunch of other lies you are going to believe after that.

It's all rooted in the refusal to acknowledge that we are all interconnected, that our actions have consequences outside ourselves, that we depend on each other and on natural forces completely outside of our control to survive.

@mdavidallen This mornings related reading https://t.co/nRIpig2tnr

@mdavidallen The internet was built by really smart people to be malleable, and it will only get better if every single one of us start finding ways to contribute to making it better or creating better online communities and structures. I’m doing a lot of research on how to best do that atm.

@mdavidallen I’m not saying the criminal justice system is in any way suited to police the internet- it is not- but humans are fully capable of designing systems to combat these situations. The difference is we treat media companies like feudal lords to be bargained with.

@mdavidallen In the real world we have actual Judges (again, good and bad) whose dedicated purpose is to settle disputes and stop bad actors from disrupting communities. Different communities have different ordinances they enact depending on their preferences.

@mdavidallen We also make our own police forces though (good and bad) - one of the most interesting things to me is the internet as a lab for self governance in a relatively post-scarcity world (creating and moving bytes is becoming so cheap as to be free) - we can design & create better
Maybe having humans with ethical, moral, and editorial judgements at the end of the firehose of all electronic manifestations of thought isn’t such a terrible idea after all
